growth_records.cite: Kulacki, Konrad J. et al. Effects of Nano-Titanium Dioxide on Freshwater Algal Population Dynamics. PLoS ONE (2012). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0047130. [PMC3468504]
growth_records.snippet: The maximum specific growth rate (day-1) was calculated as the largest proportional increase in biomass measured between any two consecutive sampling days ... [Anabaena spp. GRmax-vs-nTiO2 intercept = 0.340 day-1].
growth_records.notes: Anabaena spp. is a cyanobacterium (prokaryote). Control-condition (0 mg/L n-TiO2) maximum specific growth rate = regression intercept of GRmax-vs-[n-TiO2] = 0.340 day-1 (Table 2). Converted to per-hour: 0.340/24 = 0.01417 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.340 = 2.038 day = 48.9 h. GRmax is fluorescence/chlorophyll-a-based, genus-level, single replicate per treatment, taken as a regression intercept -> low confidence. No uptake/secretion rates measured. Medium = soil extract (potting-soil steep + MgCO3); detailed chemistry in Table S1 (not in main text).
